COMBAT
Hien levels Fatigue will cause character t0 fall uncon- scious: character $ Fatigue exceeds twice his WIL_ he I5 overwhelmed by fatigue and falls unconscious (see 116).
Stun Any wound effect beyond Fatigue Only will result in the char- acter being stunned_ This effect takes place at the end of the phase in which he suffered the wound, stunned character can: not move (and considered to have stood still regardless of his last Movement Action attacked) make attacks until spends Simple Action t0 recover from being stunned and he suffers +2 TN modifier to all actions he must make while stunned such as defending himself in melee combat; stunned character does not suffer any additional effects if he stunned again before he recovers_
Recoverlng Fatigue Fatigue can be recovered during combat by spending Complex Action This recovers 106 Fatigue (to minimum of Fatigue). If the situation is dire character can spend an Edge point and Complex Action to eliminate all of his Fatigue with no die roll required:
WOUND EffECTS Wounds represent serious injuries that can cripple or kill character; The more wounds character suffers tne more like Iy it is he will suffer permanent injury or die Each wound type has wound value assigned t0 it (see the Wound Table 1141. This represents the relative severity tne wound and helps determine character' overall condition. Every wound character takes inflicts point of Fatigue on the character. Other specific effects of each type of wound are described in the following sections_ Glass Jaw: Characters with the Glass Jaw Trait add to the wound value of all wounds except the Fatigue Only result, which still has value of Pain Reslstance: Characters with Pain Resistance are not stunned by Grazing Wounds, and the total TN modifiers for all wounds taken by the character are reduced by For example_ character with Pain Resistance who has suffered Minor Wounds and Serious Wound would have total TN modifier of rather than +4 Toughness: Characters with the Toughness Trait reduce the wourid value ot all wounds by This has no effect on the wound value increases from bleeding:
Knockout the character suffers Serious Wound Or worse, he might be overcome by pain or shock and knocked out. The target must make Knockout Check at the end the phase in which he was wounded This i5 standard WIL Attribute Check with base TN of 15 minus the character'$ WIL plus the target' $ Fatigue in excess of his WIL (including the Fatigue just inflicted by this attack) Also apply TN modifiers for any wounds the char- acter has suffered (including the wound just inflicted by this attack) as usual. No other modifiers apply to this If this Check is failed the character knocked out and falls uncon- sciouS immediately; Obviously; unconscious character cannot move Or take any Otner action: completely immobile and will not wake up on his Own (see Unconsciousness above).
previousiy unwounded character   with WIL takes Serious Wound,  forcing hum make Knockout Check: The base TN for this is (15 9). The character has Only suffered point of Fatigue S0 Iar; the inflicted by this attack; this does not exceed his WIL, there no modifier t0 the TN for Fatigue. Serious Wounds add +2 to all TNs, So this is added for final TN 0f 11 The player rolls which good erough to keep him the fight:
Unconsclousness tne tota wound value of all wounds suffered by the char acter exceeds his BOD Attribute danger = of dying: He will fall unconscious unless his WIL Attribute iS higher than his BOD Attribute in which case he can force himself to stay awake until the total wound value exceeds his WIL_
Knockdown (Optional) stands to reason that powerful attacks would have chance to knock character off his feet: However; since this can add little complexity and bog down combat; it present optional rule Some special melee   maneuvers weapons will cause knockdown; those effects are not optional: Checking for knockdown for other kinds of attacks is optional, however; the players and gamemaster should all agree whether Knockoown rules will be used their campaign_ the number of damage dice rolled for an attack (not including extra dice rolled for open ended success_ but including extra dice for Burst Fire, see 118) exceeds the target's BOD minus his current Fatigue _ there is chance the attack will knock him off his feet: After such an attack is resolved_ the target must make Knockdown Check: This resolved as an Action Check using the total damage rolled for the attack minus the target'$ STR as the base target number the target has Acrobatics Skill, he
The Bleeding Effect Once unconscious the character does not receive suC cessful treatment with First Aid Skill within minute   of reaching this condition_ the wound value of his most serious wound increases by This bleeding effect continues every minute until the character receives treatment;
Dying from Wounds If the total wound value of all wounds suffered (including tne added wound value for ~bleeding" ever exceeds the char- acter $ BOD WIL_ dies unless first  aid is immediately applied and more advanced medical care is promptly available (see Emergency Care. 1231.
